MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-24-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Don Willenburg of GORDON & REES LLP (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party Hennessy Industries, Inc.
Oren P. Noah of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Leonard Shields and Janice Shields

RULING:
DEFENDANT HENNESSY INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden. The declaration of Rudy Limpert creates a triable issue whether defendant is liable for Mr. Shields' alleged exposure to asbestos attributable to defendant's Ammco brake grinding machine. While such machine itself did not contain asbestos or asbestos-containing component parts, there is a triable issue whether defendant owed a duty to Mr. Shields. Plaintiff's objections to the declarations of Craig Mountz and Russell Darnell are overruled. Defendant's objections to Rudy Limpert's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Holly Sayers of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13678 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery
Off Calendar Feb-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shelley K. Tinkoff, Esq. of BRYDON HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for defendant / moving party Toyota Motors Sales, U.S.A., Inc.
Gibert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Leonard Shields and Janice Shields
Lynn R. Fiorentino, Esq. of NIXON PEABODY (415-984-8412), appearing for defendant / joinder Mack Trucks, Inc.
Richard R. Ames, Esq. of CARROLL, BURDICK & McDONOUGH 415-989-5900, appearing for defendants / Joinders VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC. (fka Volkswagen of America, Inc.) and Reynold C. Johnson Company

RULING:
DEFENDANT TOYOTA MOTORS SALES, U.S.A., INC.'s Motion To Compel Discovery. Argued and the Court granted Defendant's motion to compel discovery. Plaintiffs are to produce to Berry & Berry plaintiff's pathology within 10 days. The pathology material will be returned to plaintiffs' counsel in March. Joinders' requests to compel are denied. No one can conduct any destructive testing. Toyota must provide plaintiff with a pathology report prepared by its expert and serve it timely on everyone. If in good cause plaintiff decides to depose Toyota's expert, the costs will be born by Toyota. Counsel for Toyota will notify Berry and Berry and cause them to inform all defendants in Asbestos Litigations that going forward all pathology material will be returned to plaintiff's counsel no later than 4 months prior to the inital trial date. This only applies to non-preference cases. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne T. Acuna, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Leonard Shields, et al.
No other appearances.

RULING:
Defendant Orchard Supply Hardware LLC's motion for summary judgment. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Declaration of Leonard Shields creates triable issue of fact of exposure to asbestos containing tile purchased from Orchard. Orchard's objections to the declaration are overruled. Summary adjudication as to cause of action for false representation granted. Orchard has shifted the burden of production with factually devoid discovery responses and Shields produces no evidence of any representation made by Orchard to Shields. Order signed in open court. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2010 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: DEFENDANT FRICTION MATERIALS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is off calendar per request of moving party. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Susan J. Lee, CSR # 4280

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Anne T. Acuna, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Leonard Shields, et al.
No other appearances.

RULING:
Defendant Utility Trailer Manufacturing's motion for summary judgment. No argument was made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant's motion is denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of production with respect to the issues of exposure to original brakes on a Utility Trailer, the asbestos content of those brakes, causation, and whether Mr. Shields suffers from an asbestos-related disease. Defendant did not propound "all facts" interrogatories and Mr. Shields testified at deposition that he removed original brakes from Utility Trailers. He was not asked if those brakes contained asbestos. Mr. Shields provides a sufficient foundation for his statement that the brakes were original. Defendant's objections to his testimony are overruled. Mr. Stanton's declaration that the brakes "may or may not have contained asbestos" is insufficient to negate an element of plaintiffs' claims. Mr. Shields' deposition alone does not show that he cannot reasonably obtain information that he suffers from an asbestos-related disease. The deposition testimony of Dr. Douglas Freeman does not negate this element because he does not know if there is a correlation between nonsmall cell carcinoma and asbestos exposure and does not know if Mr Shields' atelectasis was caused by asbestos exposure. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Susan J. Lee, CSR # 4280
